title: Heavy rain likely in Odisha, Bengal; heatwave to persist in parts of south India - CNBC TV18
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
full_text:
2 Min Read
Heavy to very heavy rainfall is likely over parts of Odisha and Gangetic West Bengal on Thursday, July 16, as a low-pressure area over the northwest Bay of Bengal and adjoining north Odisha-West Bengal coasts strengthens rainfall activity across eastern India, according to the India Meteorological Department (IMD).
It is likely to become more marked and move northwestwards across north Odisha and Gangetic West Bengal over the next two days.
In the northeast, widespread rainfall is forecast across Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Meghalaya, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram and Tripura. Isolated heavy rainfall is likely over Assam and Meghalaya, as well as Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram and Tripura.
Rainfall activity is expected to remain relatively subdued over west-central and south peninsular India. However, Konkan and Goa are likely to see fairly widespread to widespread showers, while isolated to scattered rainfall is forecast over Gujarat, Madhya Maharashtra and Marathwada.
Meanwhile, heatwave conditions are likely at isolated places over coastal Andhra Pradesh, Rayalaseema and Tamil Nadu, Puducherry and Karaikal on July 16. Hot and humid conditions are also expected in Delhi, Haryana, Punjab and parts of Uttar Pradesh.
Delhi is expected to see a partly cloudy but hot and humid day, with the maximum temperature ranging between 37°C and 39°C and the minimum between 27°C and 29°C.
It is likely to become more marked and move northwestwards across north Odisha and Gangetic West Bengal over the next two days.
The IMD has forecast isolated very heavy rainfall over Odisha on July 16 and 17, while heavy rain is also likely in parts of Jharkhand, Bihar and Chhattisgarh. Widespread rainfall is expected across Gangetic West Bengal, Jharkhand and Odisha, with the wet spell extending across much of east and northeast India.
In the northeast, widespread rainfall is forecast across Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Meghalaya, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram and Tripura. Isolated heavy rainfall is likely over Assam and Meghalaya, as well as Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram and Tripura.
Rainfall activity is expected to remain relatively subdued over west-central and south peninsular India. However, Konkan and Goa are likely to see fairly widespread to widespread showers, while isolated to scattered rainfall is forecast over Gujarat, Madhya Maharashtra and Marathwada.
Meanwhile, heatwave conditions are likely at isolated places over coastal Andhra Pradesh, Rayalaseema and Tamil Nadu, Puducherry and Karaikal on July 16. Hot and humid conditions are also expected in Delhi, Haryana, Punjab and parts of Uttar Pradesh.
Delhi is expected to see a partly cloudy but hot and humid day, with the maximum temperature ranging between 37°C and 39°C and the minimum between 27°C and 29°C.
(Edited by : Tenzin Norzom)

title: Heavy to very heavy rainfall likely over Arunachal Pradesh, Odisha and Gangetic West Bengal - News On AIR
full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued an orange warning for heavy to very heavy rainfall at isolated places over Arunachal Pradesh, Gangetic West Bengal and Odisha today.
The IMD has also forecast heavy rainfall over Assam, Meghalaya, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, Tripura, Sub-Himalayan West Bengal, Sikkim and Uttarakhand today.
The weather agency has predicted isolated to scattered rainfall over Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi, East Rajasthan, West Rajasthan and West Uttar Pradesh during the next two to three days.
Heatwave conditions are also expected at isolated places over Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry and Karaikal today.
In Delhi, the Meteorological Department has forecast hot and humid conditions with a partly cloudy sky during the day.
